action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 5 trained a 512-hidden, 4-layer model for 40 epochs (~50 min). Training loss declined from 0.95 to 0.72, but validation loss plateaued around 0.366-0.372 with best at epoch 29 (0.3665). No evaluation was submitted for this checkpoint, so the mae_60min is unknown. With ~2 hours and 25 iterations remaining, I must first establish whether this larger model actually improved the primary metric before investing more time. Analysis: The previous best mae_60min was 4.2428 (256h, 3l, 20 epochs). The new checkpoint should be competitive since val_loss is slightly lower, but severe underfitting suggests architectural or optimization changes may be needed next. Decision: Submit the current best checkpoint for evaluation immediately without additional training to measure mae_60min and decide on subsequent scaling.
